question_text: Two identical light bulbs are available: bulb X rated 60 W and bulb Y rated 100 W. Both are connected to the same 230 V mains supply and left on for 24 hours. Which bulb transfers more total energy, and by how much?
correct_answer: Bulb Y transfers more energy; the difference is 34,560,000 J (or 34.56 MJ or 9.6 kWh)
explanation: Energy transferred = Power × Time. Bulb X: 60 × 86,400 s = 5,184,000 J; Bulb Y: 100 × 86,400 s = 8,640,000 J. The higher power rating means bulb Y transfers more energy in the same time, even though both operate at the same voltage.
